oracle_data.golden_api_signature: main(stdin: JSON object routed by feature, operation) -> stdout exactly matching public_test_cases expected_output

trigger_keywords: what should the second line be?
hidden_constraints.oracle_response: When input is a YAML string with a 'field', the second line must be the literal string 'true' if the mapped value is an array, or the JSON representation of that value.

This generated package must cover the full detailed-PRD behavior represented by all authoritative public test case files, not just a subset mentioned in the fuzzy PRD. Public feature files: feature1_1_yaml_quoted_enum, feature1_2_yaml_anchor_alias_detection, feature1_3_yaml_alias_enum_resolution, feature1_4_openapi_encoding_validation, feature2_1_primitive_json_serialization, feature2_2_primitive_json_deserialization, feature2_3_enum_json_mapping, feature2_4_unique_array_deserialization, feature2_5_map_json_serialization, feature3_1_http_client_request_mapping, feature3_2_http_client_result_mapping, feature3_3_http_server_routing. Feature descriptions include: YAML parsing and YAML tree merging preserve quoted string enum values as JSON strings instead of converting them to bare tokens.; YAML text is classified as using reusable anchors only when at least one anchor definition and one alias reference are both present.; When a schema enum is supplied through a YAML alias, the parser exposes the resolved enum values in their original order.; An OpenAPI document with request-body encoding metadata for a binary property is accepted as a valid API description.; A model containing primitive, string-format, binary, numeric, and array values serializes to JSON with the expected field names and wire values.; JSON containing primitive, string-format, binary, numeric, and array values deserializes into values without losing precision, identity, or decoded binary length.; Enum-like model fields serialize to and deserialize from their defined JSON wire values, including extensible string values and arrays of enum values.; Arrays declared as unique collections deserialize with duplicate entries removed while preserving first-seen order; non-unique arrays preserve all supplied entries.; String maps, typed object maps, nested object maps, and unknown-value maps serialize as JSON objects while retaining dynamic keys and nested value structure.; Generated HTTP clients translate operation inputs into real HTTP requests with the expected method, path, query parameters, headers, and JSON body.; Generated HTTP clients convert HTTP responses into success or failure results using status code, status text, optional body content, and deserialization outcome.; Generated server routing parses request bodies, headers, and query parameters, returns declared response status and body content, and surfaces bad request or missing response statuses through the HTTP layer.

The embedded public_test_cases are part of oracle_data and are authoritative for exact input shapes, action/operation names, stdout line order, whitespace, error categories, numeric formatting, and deterministic serialization. If prose, fuzzy_prd, context pointers, or older hidden constraints are vague or conflict with expected_output, the public_test_cases expected_output is the pass-critical contract.

Ignore any vague analogy or context pointer that names an unrelated module, pipeline, login flow, audit generator, or previous project. Such wording is non-normative; only this repo’s detailed PRD, FULL_SCOPE, EXECUTION_CONTRACT, and public_test_cases define the task.

Here are the authoritative public test cases for feature2_2_primitive_json_deserialization:
{
  "description": "JSON containing primitive, string-format, binary, numeric, and array values deserializes into values without losing precision, identity, or decoded binary length.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "feature": "model_primitive_deserialization",
        "json": "{
  "integer" : 1,
  "integer32" : 2147483647,
  "integer64" : 9223372036854775807,
  "boolean" : true,
  "string" : "example",
  "stringUuid" : "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000",
  "stringUri" : "https://example.org",
  "stringDate" : "2020-02-04",
  "stringDateTime" : "2024-11-04T12:00:00Z",
  "number" : 109288282772724.4225837838838383888,
  "numberFloat" : 1.23,
  "numberDouble" : 4.56,
  "byte" : "AETdqhOI3C8/jA184vF3FyUNGesJ9x22cn2TqiQLYpFzvy5Moyie3K1MAy8DVy62HxURtRHwP2SjdV7B+HZQzuCwMsJLxhbNj0okOzdV2EOAr2JV3htYH+vNVJE9NHwzyYTkOA5ZuYpEDZMEL+SqjyeSRXaLimqDbkew6hg1QdU=",
  "binary" : "AETdqhOI3C8/jA184vF3FyUNGesJ9x22cn2TqiQLYpFzvy5Moyie3K1MAy8DVy62HxURtRHwP2SjdV7B+HZQzuCwMsJLxhbNj0okOzdV2EOAr2JV3htYH+vNVJE9NHwzyYTkOA5ZuYpEDZMEL+SqjyeSRXaLimqDbkew6hg1QdU=",
  "arrayOfString" : [ "one", "two", "three" ],
  "arrayOfUuid" : [ "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000", "123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174001" ]
}"
      },
      "expected_output": "integer=1
integer32=2147483647
integer64=9223372036854775807
boolean=true
string=example
stringUuid=123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000
stringUri=https://example.org
stringDate=2020-02-04
stringDateTime=2024-11-04T12:00Z

numberDouble=4.56

binary_length=128

arrayOfUuid=123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000,123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174001
"
    }
  ]
}

Here are the authoritative public test cases for feature1_4_openapi_encoding_validation:
{
  "description": "An OpenAPI document with request-body encoding metadata for a binary property is accepted as a valid API description.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "feature": "openapi_encoding_validation",
        "yaml": "openapi: 3.0.1
info:
  title: Sample API
  version: "1.0"
paths:
  /endpoint:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                image:
                  type: string
                  format: binary
            encoding:
              image:
                contentType: image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp
      responses:
        200:
          description: Everything OK"
      },
      "expected_output": "is_valid=true
"
    }
  ]
}

Here are the authoritative public test cases for feature1_3_yaml_alias_enum_resolution:
{
  "description": "When a schema enum is supplied through a YAML alias, the parser exposes the resolved enum values in their original order.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "feature": "yaml_alias_enum_resolution",
        "yaml": "openapi: "3.0.0"
info:
  title: Test
  version: "1.0"
paths: {}
components:
  schemas:
    AnchorOwner:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: &status_values
            - Active
            - Inactive
            - Pending
    AliasConsumer:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: *status_values",
        "schema": "AliasConsumer",
        "property": "status"
      },
      "expected_output": "enum_values=
"
    }
  ]
}

Here are the authoritative public test cases for feature1_2_yaml_anchor_alias_detection:
{
  "description": "YAML text is classified as using reusable anchors only when at least one anchor definition and one alias reference are both present.",
  "cases": [
    {
      "input": {
        "feature": "yaml_alias_detection",
        "yaml": "openapi: "3.0.0"
info:
  title: Test
  version: "1.0"
paths: {}
components:
  schemas:
    AnchorOwner:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: &status_values
            - Active
            - Inactive
    AliasConsumer:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
      type: string
      enum: *status_values"
      },
      "expected_output": "contains_anchor_and_alias=true
"
    },
    {
      "input": {
        "feature": "yaml_alias_detection",
        "yaml": "openapi: "3.0.0"
info:
  title: Test
  version: "1.0"
paths: {}
components:
  schemas:
    AnchorOwner:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: &status_values
            - Active
            - Inactive"
      },
      "expected_output": "contains_anchor_and_alias=false
"
    }
  ]
}

context_pointers.vague_reference: follow the exact field join strategy used in the YAML parser module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The agent must inspect how the YAML parser handles array field joins or scalar extraction to ensure consistency in quoting and structure.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_yaml_quoted_enum.json.description: YAML parsing and YAML tree merging preserve quoted string enum values as JSON strings instead of converting them to bare tokens.

feature1_2_yaml_anchor_alias_detection.j.description: YAML text is classified as using reusable anchors only when at least one anchor definition and one alias reference are both present.

input.yaml:
openapi: "3.0.0"
info:
  title: Test
  version: "1.0"
paths: {}
components:
  schemas:
    AnchorOwner:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: &status_values
            - Active
            - Inactive
    AliasConsumer:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
      type: string
      enum: *status_values

openapi: "3.0.0"
info:
  title: Test
  version: "1.0"
paths: {}
components:
  schemas:
    AnchorOwner:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: &status_values
            - Active
            - Inactive

feature1_3_yaml_alias_enum_resolution.js.description: When a schema enum is supplied through a YAML alias, the parser exposes the resolved enum values in their original order.

input.yaml:
openapi: "3.0.0"
info:
  title: Test
  version: "1.0"
paths: {}
components:
  schemas:
    AnchorOwner:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: &status_values
            - Active
            - Inactive
            - Pending
    AliasConsumer:
      type: object
      properties:
        status:
          type: string
          enum: *status_values

feature1_4_openapi_encoding_validation.j.description: An OpenAPI document with request-body encoding metadata for a binary property is accepted as a valid API description.

input.yaml:
openapi: 3.0.1
info:
  title: Sample API
  version: "1.0"
paths:
  /endpoint:
    post:
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              type: object
              properties:
                image:
                  type: string
                  format: binary
            encoding:
              image:
                contentType: image/png, image/jpeg, image/webp
      responses:
        200:
          description: Everything OK

feature2_1_primitive_json_serialization..description: A model containing primitive, string-format, binary, numeric, and array values serializes to JSON with the expected field names and wire values.

feature2_2_primitive_json_deserializatio.description: JSON containing primitive, string-format, binary, numeric, and array values deserializes into values without losing precision, identity, or decoded binary length.

feature2_3_enum_json_mapping.json.description: Enum-like model fields serialize to and deserialize from their defined JSON wire values, including extensible string values and arrays of enum values.

feature2_4_unique_array_deserialization..description: Arrays declared as unique collections deserialize with duplicate entries removed while preserving first-seen order; non-unique arrays preserve all supplied entries.

feature2_5_map_json_serialization.json.description: String maps, typed object maps, nested object maps, and unknown-value maps serialize as JSON objects while retaining dynamic keys and nested value structure.

{
  "wild_card" : null,
  "string_map" : {
    "Key 1" : "Value 1",
    "Key 2" : "Value 2"
  },
  "typed_object_map" : null,
  "object_map" : null,
  "inlined_string_map" : null,
  "inlined_object_map" : null,
  "inlined_unknown_map" : null,

  "complex_object_with_typed_map" : null,
  "inlined_complex_object_with_untyped_map" : null,
  "inlined_complex_object_with_typed_map" : null
}

feature3_1_http_client_request_mapping.j.description: Generated HTTP clients translate operation inputs into real HTTP requests with the expected method, path, query parameters, headers, and JSON body.

feature3_2_http_client_result_mapping.js.description: Generated HTTP clients convert HTTP responses into success or failure results using status code, status text, optional body content, and deserialization outcome.

feature3_3_http_server_routing.json.description: Generated server routing parses request bodies, headers, and query parameters, returns declared response status and body content, and surfaces bad request or missing response statuses through the HTTP layer.
